name: bacopa monnieri
mechanism: Proposed mechanisms are based mainly on preclinical and translational studies: bacoside-rich extracts may influence cholinergic signaling, antioxidant defenses, and synaptic plasticity. These mechanisms are plausible but not fully established as clinical mechanisms in humans.
effects: No direct effects data. Contextual inference:
nan.. No direct mechanismofaction data. Contextual inference:
contraindications: avoid during pregnancy and breastfeeding unless advised by a clinician
Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Usually tolerated in studied doses, but gastrointestinal effects (nausea, cramping, loose stool) are common. Because long-term safety data are limited, use caution in pregnancy, breastfeeding, and in people taking multiple chronic medications.

sources.title: PubMed: The chronic effects of an extract of Bacopa monniera (Brahmi) on cognitive function in healthy human subjects

description: Bacopa monnieri (water hyssop) is a creeping wetland plant used in Ayurvedic medicine as a cognitive tonic. Human studies are generally small and heterogeneous; some report improvements in delayed recall and information processing after about 8-12 weeks, while short-term effects are less consistent.
dosage: No single evidence-based universal dose. Clinical trials commonly use standardized extract doses for roughly 8-12 weeks; use product-specific labeling and clinician guidance.

interactions: Interaction evidence in humans is limited; monitor closely when combining with sedatives, thyroid-active medicines, or anticholinergic/cholinergic drugs.
Use clinician oversight when combining standardized extracts with prescription medicines because product composition varies.
preparation: Most clinical studies use standardized oral extracts (capsule/tablet) rather than whole fresh herb. Products are commonly standardized to bacosides.
region: Native to tropical and subtropical wetlands of Asia and also distributed in Africa and parts of Oceania; cultivated more broadly for supplement use.
therapeuticUses: traditional support for mild digestive comfort
sideEffects: mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or loose stool)
summary: Bacopa monnieri is an Ayurvedic herb used for memory and attention support; small randomized trials suggest modest cognitive benefits after several weeks, but evidence quality is mixed.
